rulings.Case Title: HC 650 SACRAMENTO LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. STARCITY PROPERTIES INC. A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 8, 2020 line 3. PLAINTIFF HC 650 SACRAMENTO LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is DENIED. As to the implied contractual indemnity the moving party failed to sustain its burden and show that Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant is entitled to a judgment on these issues as a matter of law. The Court will not consider new arguments improperly brought in reply, e.g. CCP 1021.6, Paragraph 10 of the Settlement Agreement. As to the breach of covenant of quiet enjoyment/constructive eviction issues, there are triable issues of facts to be determined by a trier of fact as to the reasonableness of Defendant vacating the premises in February 2019 under the facts of this case. Prevailing party to prepare order in accordance with CCP 437c(g). = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: REZA R. DIBADJ VS. LAWRENCE K. LO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to Amended ANSWER to COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 8, 2020 line 5. PLAINTIFF REZA DIBADJ DEMURRER to Amended ANSWER to COMPLAINT is OVERRULED. Affirmative defenses are sufficiently alleged and are not uncertain. Furthermore, based on the arguments advanced in the moving papers the omission of references to specific causes of action did not impede Plaintiff's ability to understand and analyze the defenses. The Court notes that parties filed severely oversized briefs without leave of Court, without table of authorities or table of contents. Therefore, in each brief all pages of Points and Authorities after page 10 are stricken. In the future such violations by the parties or their counsel will result in the entire briefs being stricken. Matter was continued from November 20, 2020 because counsel was not feeling well. = (501/CFH)
